mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate below the natural rate tightens labor markets, boosting wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity valuations higher.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.9 at t-1mo, below estimated natural rate of 5.0-5.5
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 4% and S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), or if rank IC over a 2-year window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 0.5%, it signals the end of an accommodative cycle, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses 0.5%, the probability of positive 60-day returns exceeds 60%, or average returns are not negative with 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid retail sales growth coupled with stagnant industrial production indicates supply constraints, fueling inflationary pressures that force the Fed to tighten, hurting equities.
failure_condition: If in months meeting the trigger condition the 20-day forward SPX return is positive more than 40% of the time, or the joint signal has a rank IC below -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During early economic recoveries, strong GDP growth signals durable demand expansion, lifting corporate earnings and equity prices over the medium term.
failure_condition: If in quarters labeled as early recovery (first 4 quarters after GDP trough), the rank IC of GDPC1 growth on 20-day returns is less than 0.05 or negative.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, prompting defensive positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is positive, or less than 60% of instances produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated and the fed funds rate remains low, deeply negative real interest rates push investors into equities as an inflation hedge, driving short-term gains.
failure_condition: If months with CPIAUCSL YoY>3% and FEDFUNDS<1% produce a median 20-day return below 0%, or a positive hit rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which directly boosts revenue expectations for S&P 500 companies and rallies stocks over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=447308 (t-1mo) , high and growing from 2020 lows
failure_condition: If the 1-week forward return rank IC of RSAFS MoM growth is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or if the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing expansion defined by industrial production above its long-run trend, further output growth signals rising capacity utilization and pricing power, lifting equity valuations.
failure_condition: If during periods with INDPRO>100, the 60-day forward SPX return rank IC is below 0.04, or the proportion of positive returns is not significantly above 50% (p<0.05).
